story.title: Paste camera frame image directly from clipboard (Ctrl/Cmd+V)
story.as_a: Frigate user who just screenshotted the debug view
story.i_want: to press Ctrl+V anywhere in the app and have the screenshot load as the canvas background
story.so_that: I skip the save-to-disk and file-picker round trip entirely

acceptance_criteria: Given an image on the OS clipboard, when the user presses Ctrl/Cmd+V with the Editor tab active, then the image loads into the canvas through the same Store.setImage path as file upload, with identical dimension capture and normalization
Given clipboard content that is not an image (text, files), when paste fires, then the app does nothing and no error or warning is shown
Given focus inside a text input (Name, camera form fields), when the user pastes, then normal text paste occurs and the image-paste handler does not intercept

Given an image already loaded with polygons drawn, when a new image is pasted, then polygons are preserved (normalized coords re-derive against new dims) and a toast notes the image was replaced with Undo restoring the previous image
constraints: Listen for the 'paste' event on document; extract image via e.clipboardData.items (type startsWith 'image/'); no async Clipboard.read() permission prompts
Image decode goes through Platform.readImageFile(blob) — same adapter as upload, no second decode path
Drop-zone hint text updated to mention paste ('Drop, click, or Ctrl+V')
Handler active only while the Editor panel is visible
edge_cases: Clipboard contains both text and image (e.g., copy from a web page) — image item wins
Very large screenshot (e.g., 4K, >20MB decoded) — loads normally; canvas already sized to natural dimensions
Paste before any prior image exists — behaves exactly like first upload, placeholder hides
Animated or unsupported MIME in items list — first item whose type matches image/png|jpeg|webp is used; others ignored
risks: Low: browser clipboard quirks on Linux/Wayland — paste event path (not async API) is the most compatible route

open_questions.question: Should pasting while polygons exist warn before replacing the image?

options.label: Replace instantly + undo toast (Recommended)
options.info: Consistent with no-confirmation-dialogs principle; polygons survive anyway since coords are normalized.

options.label: Block with inline confirm
options.info: Safer-feeling but violates master spec UI principle #6.

tasks.description: Add document paste listener with input-focus guard and image-item extraction; route blob through Platform.readImageFile → Store.setImage
Add image-replaced toast with undo (snapshot previous image dataURL + dims); update drop-zone hint copy
QA: paste PNG/JPG/WebP screenshots on macOS and one other OS, text-paste passthrough in inputs, replace-with-polygons scenario

docs_md:
## Clipboard Image Paste
Screenshot the Frigate debug view, focus the app, Ctrl/Cmd+V. The pasted image becomes the canvas background via the same pipeline as file upload. Pasting over an existing image is instant with a 5s undo. Text pastes into form fields are untouched.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Single paste entry point with focus guard

metrics: Paste-to-rendered latency < 500ms for a 1080p PNG screenshot
0 interference with text paste in form fields across QA matrix
100% parity of dimension/normalization metadata between paste path and upload path (same Store.setImage call site)

decision_log.rationale: Paste event API chosen over async navigator.clipboard.read() because it requires no permission prompt, works on file:// origins, and fires with user intent. Routing through Platform keeps the Electron clipboard swap (v0.3) one-function.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: navigator.clipboard.read() — permission prompts and flaky file:// support
Rejected: dedicated Paste button — extra UI for what a global shortcut does better
tradeoffs: No paste support if browser blocks clipboardData on paste events (none of the evergreen targets do)
decision_log.confidence_rationale: Smallest spec in the batch; well-trodden web API; single integration point.

defaults_applied: Assuming paste applies to the Editor tab only; Camera Builder has no image surface
Assuming first matching image item wins when clipboard holds multiple representations
